Ad Text:
Company Description


Nine is Australia’s largest locally owned media company. Working at Nine, you’ll have access to a unique range of experiences and opportunities, helping drive the success of the country’s most trusted television, radio, digital and publishing brands. 

Our content reaches almost every Australian - meaning what we do has real impact. We bring people together, celebrate the big moments, and capture the everyday ones.

Some of our most beloved brands have been part of Australian life for generations, and others - new on the scene - have already found their place firmly in our lives. We’re evolving and we need people like you to bring new ideas, innovate and make your mark.

Job Description


Nine is seeking a Head of Delivery to lead the execution of a multi-year digital transformation: the Integrated Consumer Platform. You will act as the "operational engine," transitioning the business from brand-specific silos to a unified, horizontal ecosystem. Partnering with a Senior Program Manager, you will translate strategic goals into disciplined, predictable delivery. This role is based in our Sydney office.

What You'll Be Doing:

Strategic Execution: Map delivery workstreams to business case milestones and ROI targets.

Dependency Management: Orchestrate complex integrations across Data, Ad-Tech, Engineering, and Marketing.

People Leadership: Lead and upskill a team of Delivery Managers, moving them from "scrum masters" to "delivery owners."

Operational Excellence: Use data-driven metrics (Flow, Lead Time) to optimize workstreams and provide executive-level reporting.

Pragmatic Governance: Implement "fit-for-purpose" delivery frameworks, balancing agile flexibility with the rigour needed for major migrations.

Qualifications


What You Bring:

Proven Track Record: Extensive experience delivering large-scale software in high-traffic, complex environments.

Transformation Experience: History of working with PMOs to execute formal business cases or digital roadmaps.

Leadership: Experience managing delivery professionals and navigating organizational change.

Operational Rigour: Obsessed with optimization and using data to diagnose systemic bottlenecks.

Stakeholder Influence: Ability to manage competing priorities between Engineering, Product, and Commercial heads.

Additional Information


How we work

At Nine, our flexible work options vary by role and team. Depending on the position, this may include flexible hours, hybrid work, or part-time arrangements. We welcome discussing your flexibility needs during the hiring process - just ask the Talent Acquisition team. 

Our employee benefits include:

Access to 'Employee Exclusives' program - a way of getting closer to our incredible brands, offering unique experiences, behind-the-scenes access, and awesome perks.
Digital newspaper subscription to our mastheads.

More info at Nine Careers.

Our Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion:

We're committed to a safe, respectful and inclusive Nine. From day one, you'll be encouraged to bring your whole self to work and will be supported to perform at your best.

We encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, people with disabilities, and of all ages, genders, nationalities, backgrounds and cultures as we recognise the importance and value of diverse perspectives. Should you require any adjustments to the recruitment process, please advise us when you apply.

Work rights: Please note to apply for this role you must already have the right to lawfully work and live in Australia.

As a Team Lead Business Analysis you will support the Senior Manager Business Analysis in managing and leading the RAC Business Analysis capability, adapting and applying standard business analysis practises to support the delivery of business value.

This will starts as a 12 month max term opportunity initially.

What will you be doing?

Lead the successful management and delivery of analysis for complex initiatives, including the identification, assessment, implementation and embedding of fit-for purpose solutions.
Liaise and collaborate with key stakeholders to grow trusted relationships that support alignment with strategic goals.
Encourage networking, knowledge sharing and collaboration across the Business Analyst community.
Lead people - support the recruitment of the right talent, manage performance, and support career development, including mentoring and coaching junior staff in the practise of Business Analysis.
Work closely with Senior Manager Business Analysis to support analysis and resource planning, monitoring, and estimation.
Plan and deliver business process, system and change initiatives including managing schedules, tasks, stakeholders and change.
Lead specific work packages end-to-end, including understanding current and future states, engaging relevant business representatives, producing deliverables and providing input into change and communications planning and delivery.
Manage requirement traceability across all requirement levels (Business Needs, Business Requirements, Stakeholder Requirements, Solutions Requirements, and Transition Requirements) and through the project and solution development lifecycles.
Provide recommendations for improving systems and processes, including experimenting and testing options, performing cost benefit analysis, measuring implementation success, and sharing lessons.
Assist with coordinating dependencies between initiatives, including identifying opportunities to improve both the quality and efficiency of analysis across initiatives in the RAC Project Portfolio.
Conduct analysis in a variety of business and delivery contexts, including Agile, Waterfall, and hybrid delivery environments.
Work collaboratively with business users to identify impacts of proposed solutions on people, processes and systems, and to determine the best solution or outcome.

What are we looking for in you?

Tertiary qualifications in Technology/Commerce/Business highly desirable.
Certification or qualification in Business Analysis (IIBA CCBA/CBAP or BCS BA Certifications preferred).
Extensive experience in successful delivery of complex business-led change and transformation initiatives.
Extensive experience applying a range of business analysis techniques in different business contexts.
Management and/or leadership experience in a large customer-focused organisation in similar role.
An understanding of operations, sales and/or insurance environments is highly desirable.
Post Graduate Qualification in Technology/Commerce/Business (or progression towards) is desirable.
Additional certification covering at least one Business Analysis perspective or aligned discipline is desirable (e.g. IIBA CDA/AAC/CCA/CPO, BCS Certifications, ISC2, TOGAF, Business Architecture Guild CBA, PRINCE2/PMP, UX Design etc.).

What can we offer you?

A workplace culture where our people feel valued, respected and empowered
Work in flexible ways that meet work/life commitments and support wellbeing
Professional career development and training
Free Roadside Assistance and discounts on RAC products and services including Insurance, Holiday Parks and Resorts, Security, Finance and Auto Services
Be part of a purpose led organisation that gives back to the WA community
Opportunities for community engagement and corporate volunteering
A very active Social Club - join one of the most active social clubs in Perth with over 900 members

Position Purpose:

The Principal Digital Engineer provides expert technical leadership and strategic digital advisory services across WSP’s major infrastructure, water, transport, energy, defence and government programs.

Operating with high autonomy, the role balances hands‑on digital engineering delivery with high‑value advisory services including digital strategy, data governance, digital twins, system integration and organisational uplift.

This role is a national technical authority, helping clients and project teams design, adopt, and put digital systems and data‑centric practices into practice across the asset lifecycle, to shape organisational digital policy, methods, and capability.

Main Activities and Responsibilities:

Digital Strategy, Advisory and Client Leadership

Lead strategic digital advisory engagements for government, utilities, defence and asset owner clients, providing authoritative guidance on digital strategy, information governance, digital twins, system integration, and lifecycle data requirements.
Translate complex digital concepts into clear roadmaps that support client decision making and organisational change.
Act as a trusted technical advisor, maintaining strong relationships with senior stakeholders and aligning digital approaches with client goals and constraints.
Shape digital elements of bids, proposals and pursuits, ensuring solutions demonstrate value, differentiation and technical credibility.
 

Technical Leadership in Digital Engineering Delivery

Define and champion digital engineering standards, modelling requirements, information specifications and digital assurance frameworks across multidisciplinary projects.
Provide expert guidance on BIM execution, model coordination, metadata structures, data governance and the configuration of common data environments.
Oversee complex digital workflows including federated modelling, design coordination, constructability sequencing, logistics simulations and integration with project and client systems (e.g. Engineering/Document Control, Project Controls and Asset Management Systems).
Conduct high-level technical review and assurance of digital deliverables to ensure compliance with client and organisational requirements.

Delivery Enablement, Problem Solving and Digital Risk Management

Resolve digital engineering and information management challenges impacting project delivery, applying structured problem solving, analytical methods and technical insight.
Identify digital risks early, develop mitigation strategies, and ensure quality and consistency across modelling, data structures and workflow integration.
Advise project directors and discipline leads on optimal digital delivery configurations, governance models and workflow efficiencies.

Practice Leadership, Capability Development and Innovation

Lead the development of guidance, frameworks, templates and tools that strengthen WSP’s national digital engineering capability.
Mentor practitioners across engineering, BIM, GIS, data and digital delivery disciplines, fostering capability uplift and consistent technical standards.
Monitor emerging technologies, standards and policy settings, translating industry trends into practical innovation pathways for projects and clients.
Represent WSP in industry forums and digital policy discussions, supporting the organisation’s market presence and technical influence.
 


Qualifications (Desirable):

Bachelor’s Degree (Engineering) or equivalent
 

Experience & Skills:

10–15+ years’ experience in digital engineering, digital advisory, information management or related technical leadership roles across infrastructure, transport, water, energy, defence or government sectors.
Deep expertise in digital engineering governance, modelling standards, information requirements, data structures and model assurance.
Strong capability in configuring, governing and assuring integrated digital delivery environments (e.g. Autodesk Construction Cloud, ProjectWise, InEight Document, Aconex, SharePoint‑based environments).
Demonstrated experience integrating digital workflows across planning, design, construction and asset operations, including alignment with client asset‑information requirements.
Proficiency in workflow automation or scripting tools (e.g. Python, Dynamo, Power Automate) that support efficiency and data quality.
Proven ability to lead multidisciplinary digital scopes, resolve complex technical issues and provide authoritative guidance on digital engineering matters.
Experience shaping digital strategies, asset‑information frameworks, digital‑twin approaches or enterprise‑level information governance models.
Strong written and verbal communication skills, with an ability to influence, engage and guide senior stakeholders and technical teams.

Lead our virtual CFO offering and drive high-impact advisory outcomes across Australia and New Zealand.

Following an internal promotion, we are seeking a Business Performance Lead (VCFO) to drive our virtual CFO service. You will lead the daily operations of the Business Performance team to ensure our advisory services are scalable, standardised, and commercially sustainable. Your focus will be on delivering high-impact strategic and financial planning for senior, high-trust client engagements. 

This opportunity is open to candidates across Australia, with the ability to work from any of our regional or metropolitan hubs.

Your role, your contribution

Client Delivery Leadership: Oversee the delivery of the VCFO offering, ensuring consistent quality and scope discipline. You will personally lead Strategic Planning workshops and senior advisory engagements such as board reporting and growth initiatives.
Operational Management: Own the weekly operating cadence including work allocation, WIP control, and quality checks. You will manage the pipeline and resolve resourcing constraints to ensure timely delivery.
Commercial Ownership: Manage a profitable portfolio by overseeing engagement economics, margins, and fee estimations. You will play a key role in proposal development and shaping solutions for new clients.
People Leadership: Provide direct coaching and mentoring to the Business Performance team. You will focus on uplifting consulting capabilities and developing Senior Client Managers into senior advisory practitioners.
Standardisation and Improvement: Drive the use of standard templates and methods to ensure delivery is repeatable and not dependent on individual preference. You will partner with the Head of Products and Services to implement automation and tools.
Stakeholder Integration: Build strong relationships with Partners and Client Teams to ensure seamless handoffs and clear communication throughout the sales and delivery process.

The benefits of joining us

Flexible working arrangements through our flexible working policy.
Option to purchase additional leave to support life commitments and planned breaks.
Structured leadership programs and development pathways, with coaching and learning support.
Strong internal networks and support across Findex to help you succeed in the role.

What you bring to the role

You are an experienced advisory professional from a public practice background who enjoys both high-level strategy and the operational mechanics of leading a team. You possess:

8 or more years of experience in professional services (accounting, advisory, or consulting) with significant SME advisory exposure.
Proven expertise in designing and facilitating advisory workshops and delivering strategic outputs.
A strong technical grounding in management reporting, three-way forecasting, and scenario modelling.
Demonstrated leadership skills with a focus on coaching and uplifting the consulting capabilities of others.
The ability to influence stakeholders and partners within a matrix structure.
A Bachelor of Business or an equivalent tertiary qualification.

Our Impact: Why Findex?

At Findex, we help clients manage, grow, and protect their financial future with confidence. Since 1987, we’ve become a leading Australasian provider of integrated accounting, advisory, and wealth solutions, delivered through our “Wealth Accountant” and Family Office models.

For the Community: The Findex Community Fund (FCF) is dedicated to giving back. Since 2018, we have donated over $4.3 million to more than 800 charities across Australia and New Zealand.
For You: We focus on developing our people first. With structured training and development opportunities, we allow our people to grow their career in a way that sets them up for success.
For our Customers: We champion the success of over 75,000 Australasian businesses, helping them grow with expert advice, fresh thinking, and top-tier professional services.
For Diversity & Inclusion: Findex is an equal opportunity employer. We value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.

Senior Test Analyst - CRM

Contract to December 2026 with possible extension | Brisbane CBD | Hybrid working

We are partnering with a leading Queensland organisation to recruit an experienced Test Analyst for to support three key streams within an enterprise CRM implementation project.

In this role you will support the Test Manager through System Integration Testing (SIT), User Acceptance Testing (UAT), and non-functional testing for both the Integration and CRM streams.

Key Responsibilities

Design, prepare, and execute test cases for System Integration Testing (SIT) and User Acceptance Testing (UAT) across the CRM and Integration streams

Validate integration points, data flows, and end to end business processes, ensuring they meet functional requirements.

Review System and Unit Test outputs provided by the implementation partner to confirm readiness for SIT and identify any gaps requiring follow up.

Identify, document, and manage defects discovered during SIT and UAT, ensuring accurate tracking and timely retesting.

Support preparation of test data and environments required for all testing phases led by LAQ.

Collaborate with business SMEs during UAT, assisting with test execution, clarifying expected outcomes, and coordinating defect triage.

Contribute to test reporting, including daily testing progress, defect trends, and test completion documentation.

Work closely with the Test Manager to ensure testing activities align with project schedules, quality standards, and agreed entry/exit criteria, especially when transitioning from partner led testing.

What we’re looking for?

Proven experience testing large-scale CRM implementations (e.g., Dynamics 365 or Salesforce).

Strong ability to test API integration points, data migration flows, and end-to-end business logic.

Ability to review external partner outputs and identify risks before transitioning to SIT.

Experience in defect lifecycle management and working within strict entry/exit criteria.

Ability to translate technical defects into business impacts for SMEs and project stakeholders.

Why apply through Lime Recruitment?

Enjoy two pay runs per week without contractor fees. As a Queensland-owned agency, we offer personalised service and are a Tier 1 supplier to the Queensland Government. Awarded Australia’s top-performing small agency in 2021 and 2022, with a finalist spot in 2023. Partner with Lime for genuine, experienced, friendly recruiters dedicated to securing the best opportunities for you. Apply now or contact  Daniel Keyte on daniel@limejobs.com.au to find out more!

As a Business Strategy Senior Specialist, you will apply your exceptional analytical and communication skills to solve complex business problems, including facilitating decision making around strategic priorities. Your ability to spot and analyse new and emerging market trends allows you to be a key contributor in solving critical business issues at Telstra.

You will be involved in defining the next wave of growth for our business. As Telstra has a centralised strategy function, you will have the opportunity to work across several interesting areas of the business, including: Telstra Consumer, Telstra Business, Telstra Enterprise, Product & Technology, and/or Telstra International. You will also work on a wide breadth of strategic project types.

Your strength in teamwork supports you in collaborating with a range of stakeholders and building strong relationships with senior stakeholders across the business.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:

You will bring your role to life by:

Solving complex problems - Actively leverage knowledge, commercial acumen, and strategic expertise to solve complex problems and provide input into decision making with the broader team to drive the development and articulation of a clear strategic direction for Telstra


Creating data-driven strategies - Utilise data and analytics to understand commercial problems, extract and communicate meaningful information, and help uncover the root cause of issues to develop data-driven strategies
Developing and articulating clear strategic direction - Drive the thinking around Telstra's long-term strategy, leveraging a breadth of knowledge to develop long term plans, formulating new strategic frameworks as needed
Analysing emerging trends and translating insights into actionable strategies - Leverage substantial expertise and experience in designing actionable business strategies to analyse new and emerging trends and evaluate their impact on Telstra's long-term needs
Engaging and influencing key stakeholders - Collaborate with and present to multiple stakeholders, including peer group and senior executives, seeking support, direction, and buy-in to generate solutions to complex problems and/or remove organisational barriers as required.
Effectively translating and communicating key information - Prepare decision making materials for the CEO, their leadership team and the Board (e.g., papers, workshops and war games) to drive senior engagement and draw out insights to achieve Telstra's strategic objectives.

ABOUT YOU:

Your leadership skills and strategic expertise see you work with complex stakeholder groups at various levels and across business unit. In doing so, you play the role of trusted advisor to senior stakeholders, including our C-suite, the CEO, and the Board.

In addition, you will have:

Strong Management Consulting and/or strategy experience
A strong ability to apply solid business acumen and be commercially astute
Strong financial and P&L knowledge
Exceptional stakeholder engagement and communication skills, with an ability to build credibility with senior leaders and executive stakeholders
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, and the ability to build sustainable, scalable solutions and processes to meet complex strategic problems

We fully embrace flexibility and choice at Telstra, and it is as unique as you. We believe every role can flex in some way and encourage you to work where, when, and how you are most engaged, safe, and productive. Talk to us about how this role could be flexible for you!

We are committed to building a diverse and inclusive workforce in all its forms. We encourage applicants from diverse gender, cultural and linguistic backgrounds and applicants who may be living with a disability.

To learn more about how we support our people, including accessibility adjustments we can provide you through the recruitment process, visit tel.st/thrive.

Job Title: Senior Data Engineer — Data Platform Owner
Ad Text:
About the Role

We are seeking an experienced and driven Senior Data Engineer to work with one of our key clients in a largely embedded manner, to own, operate, and continuously improve their cloud data platform — with Snowflake at its core.

This is not an “order-taking” role; We are looking for someone who takes genuine ownership of the platform, proactively identifies opportunities for improvement, drives initiatives from conception through to delivery, and engages directly with the business to translate needs into scalable data solutions.

You will manage a team of analysts and engineers, coordinate delivery outcomes, and be part of the primary decision-making process as well as primary escalation point for everything that sits within their data platform boundary — including ingestion, transformation layers (SAL/CAL/BAL), platform infrastructure, and cost governance. Working closely with internal solution architects to ensure strategic and long-term focused solutions are designed and delivered.


Key Responsibilities

Platform Ownership & Governance

Own the end-to-end Snowflake data platform — architecture, performance, security, and cost

Manage Snowflake resource monitors

Govern platform costs and actively optimise spend

Maintain platform documentation

Define and enforce data engineering standards

Lead platform upgrades

Data Architecture & Modelling

Design and maintain the three-layer data architecture: SAL (Staging/Acquisition Layer), CAL (Conformed/Cleansed Layer), and BAL (Business Analytics Layer)

Ensure structural integrity and data quality across all layers

Drive the evolution of the platform architecture in alignment with business growth

Pipeline Engineering & Integration

Build, manage, and maintain ELT/ETL pipelines ingesting data from source systems such as including NetSuite, Shopify and others via Celigo integration middleware

Troubleshoot, monitor, and resolve pipeline failures; own SLA compliance for data freshness

Implement data quality testing and observability frameworks

Stakeholder Engagement & Business Partnership

Work directly with business stakeholders across Finance, Operations, Marketing, and the Reporting & Analytics team to understand data requirements and translate them into platform solutions

Partner with the Reporting & Analytics Manager (Power BI) and Integration Manager (Celigo) to ensure seamless end-to-end data flow

Team & Contractor Management

Manage a team of data analysts and engineers; assign work, review outputs, and drive outcomes

Define scope, acceptance criteria, and delivery timelines for contractor engagements

Conduct performance feedback and contribute to contractor renewal decisions

Foster a high-accountability, delivery-focused team culture

Initiatives & Continuous Improvement

Proactively identify gaps, inefficiencies, and opportunities across the data platform and pipeline estate

Propose, scope, and lead improvement initiatives without requiring detailed direction from above

Evaluate and onboard new tooling, frameworks, or platform capabilities where they add material value

Contribute to the data strategy and longer-term technology roadmap




Skills & Experience

Essential

5+ years of hands-on data engineering experience in a production environment

Proven experience owning and operating a cloud data warehouse platform end-to-end — AWS Redshift, Azure Synapse, Google BigQuery, or similar

Strong SQL expertise: query optimisation, window functions, CTEs, performance tuning on large-scale cloud warehouses

Solid experience designing and building ELT/ETL pipelines and managing data transformation workflows

Experience with layered data warehouse architectures (staging, conformed/cleansed, and analytics/presentation layers)

Demonstrated ability to work autonomously, drive initiatives, and make sound technical decisions without close supervision

Experience managing or coordinating contractor or analyst resources to deliver outcomes

Strong communication skills; able to engage directly with non-technical business stakeholders

Familiarity with data quality frameworks, testing, and observability practices

Proficiency in Python or another scripting language for pipeline automation

Highly Desirable

Hands-on experience with Snowflake — including virtual warehouse management, resource monitors, RBAC, Snowpipe, time travel, and query profiling

Snowflake SnowPro Core or Advanced certification

Experience with dbt (data build tool) for transformation layer management

Familiarity with integration middleware such as Celigo / integrator.io

Experience with Power BI or similar BI tooling from a data engineering perspective

Understanding of Snowflake cost management and compute optimisation strategies

Experience in a multi-entity or franchise retail/services environment

Familiarity with version control, CI/CD practices, and infrastructure-as-code concepts




Personal Attributes

Ownership mindset — takes responsibility for outcomes, not just tasks

Proactive and initiative-driven — identifies problems and solves them without being asked

Commercially minded — understands the link between data quality and business value

Collaborative but decisive — builds relationships whilst being prepared to make calls

Resilient and adaptable — comfortable operating in a fast-changing environment

Clear communicator — able to distill complex technical concepts for a non-technical audience

You will work closely with the General Manager – Commercial and senior leadership to drive growth, expand market presence, and enhance operational performance.

M&A

Job Title: AO8 Policy Manager and Research Analyst
Ad Text:
About the Role: AO8 Policy Manager and Research Analyst

Location: Brisbane, QLD

Start Date: ASAP

Contract Length: Until 30th June + possibility of extension

Pay: $123.91 p/h + super

Engagement Type: Contract

Focus: Strategic redesign of DFV, violent, and sexual offending programs

About the role:

Lead the evolution of evidence-informed rehabilitation within Queensland's custodial settings. This role requires a sophisticated blend of academic rigor and operational pragmatism to bridge global best practices with the mission of building safer communities. You will drive systemic reform by ensuring all rehabilitative services strictly adhere to the Risk-Need-Responsivity (RNR) model while maintaining an unwavering commitment to victim safety and perpetrator accountability.

Key Responsibilities:

RNR Integration: Ensure all program designs strictly match intervention intensity to offender risk levels and address dynamic criminogenic needs.
Global Benchmarking: Conduct comprehensive national and international jurisdictional scans to map contemporary legislative and program frameworks.
Evidence Synthesis: Perform specialist literature reviews of DFV and offending research, focusing on trauma-informed methodologies and emerging trends.
Program Mapping & Audit: Conduct end-to-end audits of current custodial programs, assessing their operational viability, clinical risks, and financial impact.
Modular Design: Architect a "Base + Module" program framework that allows for tailored interventions based on specific offender profiles.
Technical Scoping: Produce detailed blueprints for new models, including implementation costs, resource specifications, and delivery protocols.
Stakeholder Leadership: Manage expert input from SMEs and design engagement strategies to build consensus among competing interests.

Key Capabilities:

Deep understanding of criminology or social policy, specifically regarding the RNR framework and the complexities of domestic, family, and sexual violence.
Ability to translate specialist literature and comparative analyses into a seamless, integrated program roadmap.
Strong research background
Proven ability to coordinate with Subject Matter Experts and translate technical insights into actionable project requirements.
Expertise in producing high-level consolidated appraisals and comparative reports for executive review.
Capacity to foster a collaborative culture and respond positively to shifting project priorities.

Qualifications:

Criminology degree
Legal/law degree or equivalent
Extensive Research
RNR framework

Other Requirements:

Operational Flexibility: The incumbent may be required to work outside of normal hours and undertake travel as necessary.
Role Scope: Flexibility to undertake other duties as required outside of primary accountabilities to meet project goals

Eligibility:

Candidates must be a subject matter expert in criminology or social policy with a deep understanding of the RNR framework and the complexities of domestic, family, sexual offending and violent offending.
Also are motivated by the opportunity to apply your research expertise to real-world systemic reform in a high-stakes environment.

Benefits of Contracting with Randstad:

Access to benefits platforms with discounts at major retailers
weekly pay runs
Exclusive access to roles before they go to market
Employee assistance programme, including counselling services

If you would like to be considered for these roles, this is a great opportunity to tell Randstad about you. Please click "Apply Now" to submit your current resume.

Your New Role

Reporting to the Senior Manager, Operations Change, the Senior Associate, Operations Optimisation plays a critical role in leading and managing the end‑to‑end delivery of operational change and optimisation initiatives across the member service model and operational work chains.

This role blends project management discipline, delivery coordination, business analysis and change management, partnering closely with cross‑functional teams and vendors to translate requirements into executable plans, manage delivery milestones, and ensure initiatives are implemented safely and embedded into BAU operations.

The Senior Associate directly supports our Member Operations team by adapting operational processes to enable efficiency gains, risk reduction, product and service model changes, industry developments and regulatory obligations. Success in this role will require a strong understanding of the service model, combined with the ability to plan, govern and deliver initiatives in complex, regulated operational environments to achieve the best outcomes for members.




In this role, you'll:


Lead the end‑to‑end delivery of operational initiatives using structured project management approaches, including discovery, scoping, planning, execution, implementation and benefits realisation.
Develop and maintain delivery plans, milestones, dependencies, RAID logs and governance artefacts, ensuring clarity of scope, accountability and timelines.
Coordinate delivery across Member Operations, Technology, Risk, Member Domain, Legal and external vendors, managing interdependencies and sequencing to protect BAU outcomes.
Lead end‑to‑end reviews of operational work chains, identifying inefficiencies, delivery risks and control gaps, and translating findings into prioritised initiatives and delivery plans.
Facilitate requirements gathering, impact assessment and design workshops, ensuring business needs are clearly defined and understood prior to delivery.
Support the evolution of the service model by defining and implementing improved ways of working, hand‑offs and operating rhythms between teams. Drive integration and cutover planning when onboarding new functions, systems or services, ensuring operational readiness and smooth transition into BAU.
Provide clear, concise and regular project status reporting, including progress against milestones, impacts, risks, issues and constraints.
Contribute qualitative and quantitative analysis to prioritisation and sequencing decisions, balancing delivery capacity, regulatory timelines and member impact.
Apply robust change and implementation management practices, ensuring stakeholder readiness, adoption, training and documentation of new processes and controls.
Embed strong risk and control thinking within all initiatives, ensuring processes, policies and systems meet compliance and governance expectations and promoting a culture of risk awareness and continuous improvement.

What You'll Need

Proven experience in project management, delivery management, business optimisation or operational change roles.
Demonstrated experience planning and delivering initiatives end‑to‑end in complex operational or regulated environments.
Strong understanding of project delivery fundamentals, including scope management, scheduling, dependency management, risk and issue management and benefits tracking.
Solid business analysis capability, including requirements elicitation, process mapping, impact analysis and solution design.
Strong analytical and problem‑solving skills, with experience using tools such as Excel, Power BI, JIRA, Confluence and process mapping software (e.g. Visio).
Experience applying structured methodologies such as Lean, Six Sigma, Prosci, ADKAR, Prince2 or Agile/Hybrid delivery approaches (certification desirable).
Experience within financial services, superannuation, member‑based or highly regulated industries is highly regarded.
Excellent communication, facilitation and stakeholder management skills, with confidence engaging senior and cross‑functional audiences.
Ability to work autonomously while coordinating delivery across multiple teams in fast‑paced operational environments.
Working knowledge of systems used in Member Operations (e.g. CRM, KMS Lighthouse, Nintex) is desirable.

Our portfolio includes well-known names like Priceline, as well as our MediAesthetics brands - Clear Skincare Clinics and SILK Laser Clinics. In the digital space, we're proud to have SISU Health and InstantScripts—and most recently, we've launched our newest retail beauty stores, atomica.

What began in 1910 as a small co-operative of just three pharmacies has grown into Wesfarmers Health—a leading Australian organisation in health, beauty, and wellness.

Experience designing and implementing modern cloud-based data platforms, preferably on AWS, using Infrastructure as Code (IaC) tools like Terraform.
Lead R&D efforts, exploring emerging technologies and evaluating their applications for system enhancement.
Knowledge of machine learning concepts and their integration with data platforms.

Experience in technical leadership skills, including mentoring teams, providing strategic guidance, and shaping organisational strategies.

About the role

Zezamii is building Zezamii Vision, an AI-powered video analytics platform for real-time event detection on live camera feeds. The business is now productising the solution into a scalable platform that combines live inference, event management, client administration, and reusable machine learning pipelines. This role leads the ML side of the product across the full lifecycle, from model fine-tuning and evaluation through to deployment support and reusable training pipelines.

Initial focus

Fine-tune and improve production computer vision models for live event detection.

Increase accuracy across edge cases, difficult scenes, and extreme scenarios.

Help create a reusable Azure ML training architecture that can support future use cases and industries.

Key responsibilities

Fine-tune and improve computer vision models for real-time event detection.

Build and maintain reusable training and retraining workflows in Azure ML.

Design robust evaluation processes, metrics, and error analysis pipelines.

Work with labelled datasets, annotation feedback loops, and data quality improvement.

Support deployment of models into production inference environments.

Collaborate with platform engineers building the live streaming, event tracking, and dashboard components.

Help define the reusable ML architecture for future use cases and industries.

Contribute to model monitoring, versioning, and continuous improvement practices.

Required skills and experience

Strong experience in Python for machine learning and data workflows.

Hands-on experience with computer vision model development and fine-tuning.

Minimum 2-3 years of experience in computer vision model development.

Experience with PyTorch or TensorFlow, preferably PyTorch.

Experience with object detection, activity recognition, video analytics, or event detection.

Experience with model training, validation, experiment tracking, and performance optimisation.

Experience deploying or supporting ML models in production environments.

Minimum 1-2 years of experience in production ML model deployment.

Experience with cloud ML platforms such as Azure ML, AWS SageMaker, or GCP Vertex AI.

Strong understanding of dataset quality, false positive/false negative trade-offs, and edge-case analysis.

Preferred experience

Azure ML Studio and Azure-based ML workflows.

Live video streams, CCTV, RTSP, or real-time inference systems.

Docker, containers, and cloud deployment pipelines.

Safety-critical or compliance-sensitive environments.

Designing reusable ML systems rather than one-off models.

What success looks like

Production models achieve improved production-grade accuracy.

The training pipeline becomes reusable across future Zezamii Vision use cases.

Model deployment becomes more repeatable and reliable.

ML workflows are well-documented and scalable.

If you’re looking for a role where you can operate at scale, influence strategy and push the boundaries of paid growth, this is a standout opportunity.  Apply online or call Michelle Rubinstein at The Onset - 0413 463 043.

Grant Thornton Australia is one of the world's leading independently-owned and managed accounting, advisory and consulting firms.

About this role

We are looking for a Senior Manager to join our Risk Consulting team who will work closely with our clients to deliver practical technology risk solutions including areas such SOC-2 reporting (security, availability and integrity), cyber security, data risk / data governance, open banking accreditation, privacy and IT audit. This will be offered initially as a 6-month contract.

Key responsibilities

Lead and deliver a range of technology risk projects including SOC-2, cyber security, data risk / data governance, GS 007, open banking accreditation, privacy and IT audit.
Execute IT risk reviews against security frameworks such as COBIT, PCI-DSS, ISO 27001 and NIST
Client relationship management including planning, budget preparation and tracking, scope determination and value add
Coaching and developing a team of seniors/consultants/associates
Actively managing risk matters
Clearly articulating the risks and opportunities associated with IT to a broad range of technical and executive stakeholders
Ability to communicate and demonstrate a logical risk based approach to the review of technology
Providing clients with pragmatic recommendations to address areas of concern, risk or opportunity

Skills & experience

We’re looking for an experienced leader skilled in supporting and motivating their team through training and mentoring and fosters a culture of collaboration and inclusion. This role will suit a results driven, team orientated professional with the following skills, experience and attributes:

IT risk and security experience management experience in a private/public practice environment
Experience with auditing standards and industry frameworks such as SOC-2, GS 007, IIA/ISACA, open banking, COBIT, PCI-DSS, ISO 27001 and/or NIST
A passion for providing exceptional service and delivery to clients 
CISA, CRISC, CISM, CISSP or CA / CPA qualifications, or steps towards achieving these qualifications, will be highly regarded as will experience in a professional services environment
Ability to articulate your understanding of major IT systems and their deployment across organisations
Ability to work independently with a strong sense of ownership

And many more!

Job Title: SAP Success Factors Senior Specialist Lead Employee Central (EC)
Ad Text:
Company description:



Deloitte Services Pty Ltd



Job description:


We value in-person connection with our teams and clients, with flexibility to balance work and life
Mentoring programs - receive support and coaching to progress your career ​
Recognition culture to celebrate milestones and discounts at hundreds of retailers

Ready to level up your career? We're looking for people with SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central skills in the Senior Specialist Lead level in our practice. At Deloitte, you will work in an award-winning team of SAP SuccessFactors professionals. We are facing strong business growth, so we are expanding the team that has awarded us the #1 SF Partner by SAP for 15 years. Interested?

What will your typical day look like?

As a SuccessFactors Employee Central professional, your day-to-day you might be:

Consulting with the client, designing and building Employee Central solutions.
Lead or participate in workshops with business owners to gather requirements or validate solution.
Managing client expectations on deliverables and timelines.
Project documentation including requirements, configuration and user guides.
Facilitate training sessions for end-users or HR teams to help them understand the system.

About the team

The Deloitte HR Transformation team provides a complete range of specialised services across the world's foremost Human Resources, Payroll & Workforce Management solutions including industry leaders SAP SuccessFactors, Oracle and UKG Dimensions and we are recognised by clients and the industry as a leading provider of solutions, services and support.

Enough about us, let's talk about you.

You will have the following:

A minimum 7 years' experience with hands-on SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central implementation in a Lead position.
Hands-on configuration knowledge in SF Employee Central is essential. Configuration knowledge in SF Time Off, SF Timesheet or SF Time Tracking would be an advantage.
Project experience in implementation is imperative with proven experience in employee lifecycle implementation.
Data migration experience would be beneficial.
Professional mindset and behaviour - be responsible and responsive.
Interacting and engaging effectively through workshops and meetings with the different stakeholders and team members to deliver a world class HR Transformation solution.
Meeting your project deliverables through meeting your individual deadlines and ensuring project scope is maintained.
Strong level of experience in gathering, assessing and developing the requirements from business owners.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
Customer service focused and problem-solving approach.
An excellent communicator who is able to convey system configurations to a business language which can be understood by the business users.
A can-do attitude with a willingness to learn new functionalities, adapt to a client's changing needs and project scopes.
A team player who works well with other team members, sharing knowledge and supporting colleagues to achieve common goals.
Analytical mindset that is able to translate complex business requirements into system configurations.
Experience in working across multiple projects at any one time.

Why Deloitte?

At Deloitte, we focus our energy on interesting and impactful work. We're always learning, innovating and setting the standard; making a positive difference to our clients and our society. We put coaching at the heart of what we do, helping our people grow their careers in any direction - whether it be up, moving into something new, or even moving across the world.   

We embrace diversity, equity and inclusion. We have a diverse collection of people from different backgrounds, with different experiences, gender identities, abilities and thinking styles. What binds us together is a shared commitment to value everyone's perspective and to cultivate inclusion; so that our work environment is a safe space we can all belong.  

We value in-person connection with our clients and our colleagues. We offer several ways for you to work flexibly so that you can serve your clients, stay connected with your team, and manage your personal priorities.

We help you live and work well. To support your personal and professional life, we offer a range of perks and benefits, including retail discounts, wellbeing leave, paid volunteering days, twelve flexible working options, market-leading parental leave and return to work support package.  

Next Steps

Apply now, we'd love to hear from you!

Data Scientist - Analytics / ML

Melbourne / Sydney

$1100-1400 a day

12 month contract

Overview
We are working with a leading Financial Services client on a new role in their Data team. This role sits within a growing analytics function focused on supporting a direct-to-consumer business. The team is responsible for enabling better day-to-day decision-making through data, improving performance, and delivering actionable insights across the organisation.

You will work at the intersection of data science, analytics, and business strategy-partnering closely with teams such as marketing and digital to translate data into meaningful outcomes.

Key Responsibilities

Design and deliver machine learning models to solve business problems, with an initial focus on customer segmentation and clustering

Explore and validate multiple data sources to identify opportunities for insight generation

Translate business challenges into analytical frameworks and data science solutions

Build, test, and deploy models using tools such as Python, SQL, and Databricks

Develop reporting and visualisations to communicate insights to both technical and non-technical stakeholders

Partner with internal teams (e.g. marketing, digital) to guide targeting, performance optimisation, and decision-making

Contribute to the development of scalable data science capabilities and best practices

Monitor and track model performance, ensuring solutions are production-ready and continuously improved

Support migration and transformation of legacy analytics processes into modern data environments

Clearly explain methodologies, outputs, and impact of models to a range of stakeholders

What You'll Be Working On

Customer segmentation projects using clustering techniques

Exploratory data analysis to assess and validate available data

End-to-end model development: from discovery - build - production - monitoring

Enabling broader business use cases through incremental data science projects

Supporting the evolution of the organisation's analytics and machine learning capability

Skills & Experience

Essential:

Strong experience with Python & SQL

Hands-on experience building and deploying machine learning models

Ability to work across the full data science lifecycle

Experience working with large and complex datasets

Strong communication skills-ability to explain technical concepts to non-technical audiences

Experience working in collaborative, cross-functional environments

Desirable:

Experience with Databricks or similar cloud-based data platforms

Familiarity with BI/reporting tools and data visualisation

Experience in customer analytics, marketing analytics, or digital analytics

Understanding of clustering, segmentation, and other unsupervised learning techniques

Exposure to agile delivery environments

Salary: Strong Rates / 3 Month Contract (+ Ext) / REMOTE

Why work for Brennan?  
At Brennan, we aim to lead, not follow. One of the ways we do this is through an open diverse culture that values performance, where anyone in the team can bring new ideas to the table and see them thrive. Our people are empowered, considerate, supportive, trusting and accepting, they are the cornerstone of the business. Because of this approach, we have become the largest Australian owned systems integrator in Australia and continue to grow.  
 
Brennan offers an excellent remuneration package and benefits including:  
 

A focus on health and well-being - social club, sporting teams, health checks, poker, trivia nights and more!  
Discounted IT hardware and software products  
A strong culture underpinned by values that are truly lived every day  
An environment that embraces learning and development of all employees  
Flexibility to work in the way that brings out the best in you  
High levels of reward and recognition for overachieving 
Your role:  
 
As a Senior D365 Functional Consultant you capture, manage, and deliver project requirements, provide expertise and guidance on Brennan’s technology offerings, and guide customers through their delivery journey. This role can be based in any of Brennan’s Australian offices – Sydney, Melbourne, Adelaide, Perth, Brisbane, Canberra or Newcastle. 
 
Key Responsibilities:  
 

Work with the Project Managers to define and plan project delivery, schedules and resource requirements 
Lead client discovery session to develop an understanding of the right solution fit for their needs 
Ensure that projects are delivered as per the implementation methodology 
Ensure all work performed is of a high technical and quality standard 
Participate in the presales process accessing the clients’ needs 
Provide quality control and management oversight for all Consulting engagements. 
Provide guidance and mentoring to the consulting team that uplifts the quality of deliverables and provides proactive, constructive feedback on areas of development. 
  
To achieve success, this role requires the follow Competencies and Experience:  

Minimum 5 years’ experience in delivering Dynamics 365 Business Central solutions, specifically functional configuration experience with advanced Warehousing. 
Strong experience in successfully leading consulting engagements that have produced positive and tangible business outcomes 
Ability to develop, lead and facilitate stakeholder workshops to identify problems and develop solutions using contemporary approaches. 
Excellent interpersonal skills with a proven ability to communicate and build confidence at every level of an organisation. 
A strong client focus and a strength in taking ownership to proactively monitor customer satisfaction while negotiating mutually beneficial outcomes. 
Demonstrated knowledge of governance frameworks, risk management, project management, pre-sales and account management, strategic analysis tools (i.e. design thinking, value proposition canvas), policy, standards, business process analysis, strategic planning. 

It is also essential that you are Australia based with full working rights and preferably be a citizen.
  
Note: As part of our hiring process, you may be required to undertake a Technical Assessment and will be required to undertake a National Criminal History Check.  
 
Brennan IT is an equal opportunity employer

About Brighter Super:
At Brighter Super it’s all about the personal connections, guiding our members to retire confidently as a member first fund – we live by the philosophy that our members come first in everything we do. Since 1965, we have been a trusted and reliable provider of investment, advice and insurance services. We’re a 100% member-owned, Queensland-based, open superannuation fund, built on the foundation of three funds (LGIASuper, Energy Super and Suncorp Super) where we manage more than $37 billion in retirement savings for over 348,000 members. Our members come from our foundation industries of government, finance, electrical and energy sectors, alongside a diverse cross-section of the wider community.

Our Brighter Super team is essential to our success, embodying our core values of Aspiring for our members, Unlock innovation, Performance with purpose, and Winning together. We value every Brighter team member – Brighter people, Brighter futures, Brighter together.  This commitment fosters a supportive and collaborative work environment where each individual is empowered to thrive. Together, we are building a brighter future.
  
About your new Role: 
Your role sits within Brighter Super’s Portfolio department and reports directly to the Head of Portfolio. This role is critical to delivering fund-wide programs and cross-functional projects that will support the delivery of our strategic plan. This role is reliant on strong key business stakeholder relationships to support the development and analysis of user-centric business requirements under the EPMO at Brighter Super.

Develop strong working relationships with business stakeholders at all levels across all Business Units and Support functions.
Identify gaps and improvements to our current business analysis methodologies ensuring alignment to our Governance and delivery management frameworks, practices and processes.
Document high-level requirements, user stories and detailed acceptance criteria.
Facilitate requirements workshops with internal and external teams, showcase and UAT sessions.
Conduct training sessions with stakeholders and end users and prepare training and supporting documentation.
Raise and support mitigation and resolution of risks and issues using established tools and frameworks.
About You: 
You bring proven experience as a confident and capable Business Analyst, with experience within the Financial Services sector. Your skill set allows you to analyse complex problems, translate business needs into clear requirements, and contribute effectively to strategic and operational improvements. You are an experienced and confident BA with experience within the Financial Services Sector:

Facilitated workshops and are very confident in your own presentation skills
Created workflows in BPMN Diagram Maker
Proven ability to successfully validate and document requirements for developers and testers in form of epics, features and user stories.
Extensive experience in working in Agile environments with a cross functional team
Ability to work autonomously in an environment with shirting priorities and timelines
Why choose Brighter Super:  
We pride ourselves on being a great place to work, where you matter!  Here are some things that you will be able to enjoy; 

Workplace flexibility, with the option to work up to 50% of your week from home
Up to 18 weeks paid parental leave + super  
Purchased leave
Career pathways
Reward and recognition program
Employee Assistance Program
Holistic benefits and well-being offer through Perkbox

Ad Text:
Job description:



● Unlock meaningful insights from client data

● Experience in working across functions

● Gain skills with Australia's largest provider of government-funded hearing care

● Macquarie Park Office with hybrid work arrangements

Want to improve the lives of Australians, no matter who they are?

Join us and build a career and future you'll be proud of.

It matters more at Hearing Australia

Hearing Australia is a trusted leader in our field. Working across the country, we exist to support and empower people through hearing health when and where they need it.

Your opportunity

As Customer Data Insight Analyst, you'll be reporting to the Head of Client Value Management and will be responsible for building predictive models to forecast client behaviour and assist in the development of client segmentation models to enable personalised marketing and value propositions for different client groups. Working side by side with people who go the extra mile, you'll also love the feeling of helping children and adults who need us. Here's how you'll do it:

Deliver Data-Driven Insights to Drive Client Engagement, Retention and Commercial Impact
Develop Client Segmentation and Predictive Models to Inform Strategy
Collaborate with IT and Finance Reporting Teams to Enhance Data Infrastructure
Leverage CRM Platforms and Stay Aligned with Evolving Data Technologies
Deliver Insightful Reporting to Drive Business Decisions
Communicate Insights Effectively and Embed Data-Driven Thinking Across the Business

What You'll Bring

We believe the successful candidate will have a proven track record in driving client retention and increasing lifetime value. You'll bring:

Proven experience in extracting, analysing, and interpreting complex behavioural and commercial data to generate strategic insights and business recommendations.
Hands-on experience in campaign reporting and performance analysis, with the ability to translate data into clear, actionable insights.
Strong capability in developing client segmentation frameworks and building predictive models.
Skilled in analytics tools such as SQL, Python, R, or similar, and data visualization platforms like Power BI, Tableau, or Looker.
Strong hands-on experience with CRM platforms, ideally Microsoft Dynamics 365 and Customer Insights Journeys (CIJ), including customer data, campaign tracking, and attribution.
Relevant tertiary qualifications in Data Science, Business Analytics, Statistics, Marketing, Economics, or a related field.
Solid understanding of data quality, integration, and governance, including working with first- and third-party data sources.
Demonstrated ability to manage multiple priorities and deliver insights within tight deadlines in a fast-paced environment.

What matters most? It's simple - your drive to help anyone and everyone who needs us. And you'll see your role as a real responsibility and an incredible opportunity to make an impact on lives across the country.

Why Hearing Australia?

There's so much to love about a role with us. But our people say there's three things that set us and their careers apart. They're about:

● How we do what we do: Our vision is to deliver the best hearing solutions for anyone, anytime, anywhere. At Hearing Australia, you'll know you're making a contribution that really means something

● Who we work with: Here, you'll support and empower clients of all backgrounds. That's what drives our teams and what makes them so special

● Where we're going next: As our industry evolves, we're committed to learning and improving what we do and how we do it and building an organisation where everyone can thrive.

What does that mean for you?

As Customer Data Insights Analyst, you'll know that every day, your work is helping children and adults who need us. Just ask Sandeep, one of our IT professionals about why they choose to work with us:

"The work we do in helping people hear sound again and for the very first time is quite inspirational. Everything we do here is purpose-driven."

You'll also enjoy a range of benefits, including:

Attractive employee benefits (salary sacrifice superannuation, HCF private health cover discount, well-being programs, salary packaging, discounts across an exciting range of retailers)
Paid parental leave
Free, onsite staff parking
Employee recognition awards program
Flexible work arrangement opportunities to support work, personal and family commitments

Let's create the gold standard of hearing care

We will require the successful candidate to be willing to have a Police Check conducted (we pay), a current driver's licence and must have rights to work in Australia.

Hearing Australia is an Equal Opportunity Employer and embraces diversity, equity and inclusion. We are passionate about building a diverse and inclusive culture from within.

Ad Text:
The role:  

Reporting to the Program Director Better Hospital Flows project, you will be responsible for analysing, manipulating and presenting data.
The Queensland Government is investing in improved hospital services through real-time data and targeted technology upgrades under the Health Plan Commitment. A key priority action is Better Hospital Flows (GEC124), which focuses on modernising digital systems to improve hospital workflows, patient flow and visibility of bed availability across the state. Delivery of GEC124 is being progressed through two related initiatives:

Project 3a, which focuses on enhancements to the Statewide Management Information System (SWMIS) to improve the timeliness, quality and consistency of core operational data; and
Project 3b, which focuses on the assessment, planning and implementation of a statewide Patient Flow Insights capability that translates operational data into actionable insights.

Government Election Commitment (GEC) 124 – Better Hospital Flows – Project 3b comprises two phases: Phase 1 - Assessment and Phase 2 - Implementation.
This role relates to Project 3b: Assessment and Implementation and hereafter refers to it as the “project”.

In addition to this, you will be responsible for:

Plan and design analyses, extract, manipulate and perform statistical analysis of data using statistical software and prepare reports to disseminate information to meet the needs of the organisation.
Query missing, incomplete or inconsistent data using analysis software, including but not limited to Oracle SQL, SAS, R or Excel and undertake analysis and write queries and reports as required.
Display a wide variety of visual reports, KPI scorecards and dashboards, utilizing programs such as Power BI and Tableau.
Inspect, cleanse, transform and analyse raw data to discover useful information, inform conclusions and support effective decision making.
As required, design and build scalable data models and enabling advanced analytic capabilities that provide meaningful business value through easy-to-comprehend data visualisations.
Collaborate with key stakeholders to deliver relevant business insights based on identified business requirements.
Work independently through unstructured tasks.
Prepare confidential, complex reports, briefings and correspondence and initiate action associated with financial, procurement, human resource and other matters as required by the Program Director, Better Hospital Flows.

Are you right for this role?

To be successful in the role, you will have demonstrated capacity to analyse complex system issues and processes, provide specification/solution development and design and business case development. (NFL: Supports Strategic Direction).

Furthermore, you will have demonstrated:

Demonstrated high level of knowledge and experience in statistical methods and concepts and demonstrated ability to appropriately use statistical procedures and interpret statistical information.
Demonstrated high level of programming, data manipulation and analysis ability, including experience using statistical programming packages to interrogate, extract, and manipulate data, conduct descriptive and inferential statistical analysis and produce figures and tables; and experience with large and complex data sets and relational databases.
Demonstrated high level of interpersonal skills and written communication skills, including ability to consult with personnel at all levels in the organisation, external organisations and professional groups, ability to communicate technical information to a non-technical audience, and report writing skills.
Demonstrated ability to prioritise tasks and to effectively monitor and co-ordinate stakeholders to achieve outcomes and to meet deadlines.
Demonstrated ability to manage and develop members of a professional team in a working environment supporting quality human resource management practices.

Benefits of working with eHealth Queensland:

By joining eHealth Queensland you will work within a fast-paced, challenging and supportive environment where every employee plays a critical role in our ongoing success. 

You will enjoy a variety of benefits, including:

Competitive salary + generous superannuation and leave loading
Flexible working arrangements
Diverse work culture
Career training and development

Key role in detecting, analysing and preventing fraud and loss across the business

Hybrid working, 3 days in the office, 2 days WFH

Full time position based in either Sydney or Melbourne




We are Woolworths Group

We are Woolworths Group. 200,000+ bright minds, passionate hearts and unique perspectives connected by a shared Purpose – ‘to create better experiences together for a better tomorrow.’ It’s that Purpose that fuels our ambition to explore new ideas, make brave commitments and innovate better ways to meet the food and everyday needs of more than 24 million customers every week. 




If you’re excited to turn today’s blue sky thinking into a better tomorrow for future generations, you’ll find yourself supported and enriched in a dynamic, inclusive and empowering workplace that reflects the diverse communities we serve. With a culture of genuine care, a flexible approach to work and opportunities across the group to grow your career and make a meaningful impact, the possibilities for what we can achieve together are endless.




What you’ll do

Reporting to the Store Loss Insights Manager, the Fraud Insights Lead is a pivotal role within the Store Loss team, primarily responsible for leveraging data and technology to identify and mitigate internal and external fraud. To support the business in identifying coaching opportunities and internal/external dishonesty by utilizing advanced technology and data systems. This role packages actionable insights for investigation and store management to drive performance conversations and profit protection.

Identify New Fraud Behaviors: Proactively research and identify emerging fraud patterns and behaviors within the store environment.

AI Implementation: Leverage AI-driven methodologies to automate the detection of suspicious activities and improve the accuracy of fraud alerts.

Data Engineering: Work within the GCP (Google Cloud Platform) to develop and manage new datasets and create sophisticated rules for real-time fraud monitoring.

System Validation: Use multiple systems to validate fraudulent behaviors and quantify their impact on profit, packaging these findings for formal investigations like CCTV, POS,R10 etc.

Collaboration: Drive the overarching fraud prevention strategy by collaborating with internal and external partners to identify and fix systemic loopholes.

Stakeholder Engagement: Partner across Store Support, State Operations, and Project teams to embed collaborative initiatives and incident reduction processes.

Loss Priorities: Contribute to the development of strategic Loss priorities and support the monitoring of controls to prevent incident recurrence

Coaching & Training: Upskill store managers to conduct effective investigations and lead team member performance conversations.

Customer First: Ensure all planning and prioritisation focus on the customer while keeping processes simple and unambiguous for store teams.




What you’ll bring

Deep understanding of internal and external fraud, retail processes and supplier mechanics

Experience creating rules and datasets within Google Cloud Platform

Practical experience applying AI technologies to solve business problems

Proficient in Scan Assist, Auror, March Networks (CCTV), and store front-end systems (1 POS, Store Central, Retalix, Scan & Go)

Proficiency in Google Suite, knowledge of Sheets, Docs and Drive

High-level negotiation and presentation skills, with the ability to gain stakeholder agreement on complex decisions

Competitive salary package and bonus

Ad Text:
Important leadership role helping shape cash-related policy

Impactful work that makes a difference to the Australian people

Permanent opportunity, hybrid working

About the team

We are seeking an experienced economist or public-policy professional to join the Analysis and Policy (A&P) area of the RBA's Note Issue Department.

The Analysis and Policy section develops evidence-based policies to support the Bank's strategic priorities in relation to issuing secure and reliable banknotes, and shaping the future of money. We conduct research on cash use, access and acceptance to understand how the cash ecosystem is evolving and to identify potential policy measures to support cash as a viable means of payment. We also facilitate public-sector coordination on cash initiatives, working closely with Council of Financial Regulator (CFR) agencies and the ACCC.

A&P is leading a number of high-priority workstreams to address challenges in cash distribution.

About the role

As a manager in A&P, you will:


Provide economic and policy advice that supports priority topics and contributes to NI meeting its strategic goals, including the Bank's strategic priority to support access to physical cash.

Communicate effectively to relevant internal and external stakeholders in support of the Bank's functions and objectives.

Provide coaching and development opportunities for analysts, to support them in achieving their goals.

Contribute to the broader strategic direction and leadership of the Note Issue Department, sharing accountability with other leaders for overall performance of deliverables and outcomes.

Foster an open and inclusive culture to enable your team to deliver high quality and impactful work.

About you


Excellent critical thinking skills to guide and produce targeted analysis on complex policy issues.

Strong communication and stakeholder engagement skills.

Demonstrated people leadership skills and ability to nurture a culture of trust and collaboration in line with the Bank's open and dynamic ambition. This includes the ability to develop your team to deliver results and build skills over time.

An ability to write clearly and succinctly, with the purpose and intended audience in mind.

Strong work planning, prioritisation and organisation skills.

An ability and willingness build and maintain a culture of trust and collaboration.

Have a relevant tertiary qualification, such as in economics, finance, public policy or statistics.

Be More

Working for an organisation that truly makes a difference to the people of Australia, we can offer development and career opportunities in a collaborative environment that supports your growth, wellbeing and promotes flexibility. Your individual growth and success drives the RBA forward as an organisation. Be more means you can do more, for yourself and for Australia.

Why RBA?

The RBA makes an important contribution to the Australian economy through the pursuit of national economic policy objectives and associated activities in financial markets and banking. We also issue Australia's banknotes and operate infrastructure critical to the payments system, all of which contribute to the welfare of the Australian people. Made up of specialists across a wide range of fields, our people, values, and culture play a critical role in achieving our objectives. Striving to be Open & Dynamic, we consider and incorporate different perspectives, work across teams and are transparent with each other, whilst delivering quality together effectively and focusing on outcomes by prioritising, testing, learning, and refining as we go. Our people conduct themselves with a high degree of integrity, while striving for excellence in the work they perform and the outcomes they achieve. We encourage intelligent inquiry and we treat one another with respect while promoting the public interest through our efforts. We know it is the growth and success of our people that drives the RBA forward. Come and make a bigger contribution while you build and develop your own skills too, because being more means you can do more, for yourself and for Australia.

The Reserve Bank of Australia is committed to equity, diversity and inclusion through key initiatives. We welcome and encourage applicants from diverse backgrounds to apply, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, culturally and linguistically diverse background, those living with a disability and from the LGBTQ+ community. We are committed to making the recruitment process fair and equitable for all our candidates.
